title: senna occidentalis var. andhrica
intro: Senna occidentalis var. andhrica is a variety of the Senna occidentalis plant, a member of the Fabaceae family and a type of flowering legume native to the Americas. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative effects, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides such as aloe-emodin and chrysophanol. The health benefits of Senna occidentalis var. andhrica include its ability to relieve constipation, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, Senna occidentalis var. andhrica is often prepared as a tea, powder, or capsule, and is typically used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
